title: An innovation related to the heating system which is used in powder coating process of the temperature sensitive plates
abstract: The invention is related to the heating system (100) which can be used in electrostatic powder coating containing algorithms that provide process in typical forms, providing homogeneous heating needed in the coating process with powder of the product to be heated (120) that have temperature sensitive surfaces, carrying out heating with infra-red radiation method by which it can absorb the energy and reach chemical reaction temperatures.
claims: Claims 1. The invention is characterized in that; â¢ The heating system (100) comprises heat insulation cabinet (130) which is applicable for inlet and outlet of carrier conveyor, â¢ Heat pans (110) wherein infra-red (IR radiation) is used as a heat source within heat insulation cabinet (130), â¢ At least one contactless heat meter sensor (150) which is electronically coupled to each heating pan (110), â¢ Special algorithm that calculates radiation value analyzing raw data that is procured from heat meter sensors (150), â¢ An electronical power control card adjusting heating power according to the commands from algorithms, â¢ Graphic interface that provides adjusting and tracing of target values, â¢ Robotic heating head (140) providing cold parts to reach the deserved temperature with hot foehn by detecting heating differences on the surfaces by means of electromechanical system within, â¢ In order to remove contamination effect originated from forced air circulation, a transmission chain (160) providing hot air which arises in the top of heat insulation cabinet (130) orient to the base of the heat insulation cabinet (130), â¢ Main frame adjusting locations and angles of the heating pans (110) according to geometry of product to be heated (120). â¢ Having temperature sensitive mechanical joint with servo control which can track the surface of the product to be heated (120) in action via smart vision system, the heating system (100) is characterized in that; it includes asymmetrical heating arrangement on the purpose of providing homogeneous heating arrangement in need of coating with raw material which is the energy absorption spectrum of the products having temperature sensitive surfaces, decreases thermal instability on the surface of the product to be heated (120) without any need of forced air fans. 2. Heating system (100) which is mentioned in Claim 1 is characterized in that; the heat insulation cabinet (130) comprises of at least two sliding door systems with the intent of providing heat saving to the entries. 3. Heating system (100) which is mentioned in Claim 2 is characterized in that; the sliding doors includes an adjustable structure according to the measurements of the product to be heated (120) passing through heat insulation cabinet (130). 4. Heating system (100) which is mentioned in Claim 1 is characterized in that; heat insulation cabinet (130) has the feature of properly openable to the human entries for the maintenance inside when it is needed and passage range distance should be between 5-35 cm. 5. Heating system (100) which is mentioned in Claim 1 is characterized in that; it includes air curtain in order to make the powder flit which is coated on the product to be heated (120) with the intent of providing heat saving to the outlet of the heat insulation cabinet (130). 6. Heating system (100) which is mentioned in Claim 1 is characterized in that; heating pans (110) are located as they will be around 10-1000 pieces within heat insulation cabinet (130). 7. Heating system (100) which is mentioned in Claim 1 is characterized in that; the interface it has includes LCD panels, gradual or colorful LED neons. 8. Heating system (100) which is mentioned in Claim 7 is characterized in that; the colorful LED neons include the colors of blue, green, yellow, red and white according to their power values and radiation levels. 9. Heating system (100) which is mentioned in Claim 1 is characterized in that; heating pans (110) include an electrical or gassy structure having IR radiation. 10. Heating system (100) which is mentioned in Claim 1 is characterized in that; in order to heat the bottom of the product to be heated (120) further, with the intent of removing cooling effect of the heating pans (110) and providing same temperatures to the bottom and top portions of the product to be heated (120), the heating pans (110) are located inside the heat insulation cabinet (130) as they corresponding to portion base. 11. Heating system (100) which is mentioned in Claim 1 is characterized in that; there are panels with heater resistance in the base of the heat insulation cabinet (130). 12. Heating system (100) which is mentioned in Claim 1 is characterized in that; the heating pans (110) which are located in order to make the surface temperature distribution to be homogeneous, have the less power level between 20-250% according to the heating pan (110) on the top of the level of the heating pan (110) which is located in the base. 13. Heating system (100) which is mentioned in Claim 1 is characterized in that; in order to form an air stream with the same flow rate, with the product to be heated (120) that enter inside the heat insulation cabinet (130), the array of the heating pans (110) are asymmetrically in either angulated, delta or stereoscopical form. 14. Heating system (100) which is mentioned in Claim 13 is characterized in that; heating pans (110) of the angulated array are located with transversal angles. 15. Heating system (100) which is mentioned in Claim 13 is characterized in that; delta array comprises heating pans (110) that descendingly proceed towards the base of the heat insulation cabinet (130) for top portions of the panel plate which is the product to be heated (120) to continue to be convectively heated. 16. Heating system (100) which is mentioned in Claim 13 is characterized in that; stereoscopical array includes weak power heating pans (112) wherein the power level is between 20-250% more than the heating pan (110) on the top of the level, normal power heating pans (113) and intensive heating pans (114). 17. Heating system (100) which is mentioned in Claim 13 is characterized in that; intensive heating pans (114) in the base of heat insulation cabinet (130), normal power heating pans (113) and weak power heating pans (112) of the stereoscopical array are parallelly arrayed in order to the ground plane as they correspond at least to a line. 18. Heating system (100) which is mentioned in Claim 1 is characterized in that; it includes a smart vision system which provides to reach the desirable temperature at least in three seconds via robotic heating heads (140) wherein detection of the portions with temperature differences on the surface and via hot foehn, removes heating temperature differences on the surface and provides adjusting of the power density in the heating pans (110). 19. Heating system (100) which is mentioned in Claim 1 is characterized in that; in order to properly carry out contactless measurement and in order not to be affected by the heat in the insulation cabinet (130), it is located anywhere outside the heat insulation cabinet (130). 20. Heating system (100) which is mentioned in Claim 1 is characterized in that; it includes data accumulation system with the floating point which is integrated on the surface of the product to be heated (120) wherein heat control is provided on product to be heated (120) that progresses at a rate of 0.3-2 m/min. depending on the conveyor which is hung in heat insulation cabinet (130). 21. Heating system (100) which is mentioned in Claim 20 is characterized in that; it includes heat meter sensors (150) located on the movable head and motion perception sensors in data accumulation system with the floating point. 22. Heating system (100) which is mentioned in Claim 1 is characterized in that; it includes a data accumulation system with the fixed point wherein heat control is provided on the product to be heated (120) that progresses at a rate of 0.3-2 m/min. depending on the conveyor which is hung in heat insulation cabinet (130). 23. Heating system (100) which is mentioned in Claim 1 is characterized in that; surfaces of heating pans (110) with the scoop-like form are located as it generates air stream in the flow direction of the product to be heated (120).
